name: Conventional Deadlift
resistance_profile: Hardest breaking the bar off the floor with hips flexed — strong lengthened loading of the glutes and hamstrings — easing as you approach lockout.
cues: Push the floor away — hips and shoulders rise together
Lock out tall, then control down
execution_steps: Stand with feet hip-width, bar over mid-foot and shins close to the bar.
Hinge and bend your knees to grip the bar just outside your legs; set a flat back and brace your core.
Pull the slack out of the bar, then drive your feet through the floor — hips and shoulders rise together.
Stand tall at the top with hips fully extended, without leaning back.
Push your hips back and control the bar down along your legs to the floor, then reset before the next rep.
good_when: You want the highest-yield posterior-chain and total-body strength and mass builder in one lift.
You can hold a flat, braced spine under heavy load.
You're training for maximal strength or overall size.
bad_when: You can't keep a neutral spine under load — build up with RDLs or back extensions first.
You're already fatigued or need low-CNS accessory volume, since the deadlift is very taxing.
You have an active lower-back injury.
